Facts.text: Ajpach’ Waa, Lakam (official), dedicates his hieroglyphic stairway in the Maya city of El Palmar, commemorating his diplomatic mission to Copán for the king of Calakmul
Harun al-Rashid becomes the Abbasid caliph upon the death of his brother Al-Hadi
Stephen V's reign as Catholic Pope end with his death, succeeded a few weeks later by Pope Formosus
Pastor Frederik forms a convent at Mariengaarde, Friesland
Minamoto no Yoritomo led force of 300 defeated at Battle of Ishibashiyama in Japan
Battle at Marignano ends after two days in a French and Venetian victory over a Swiss army
Flight of the Earls from Lough Swilly, Donegal, Ireland
Treaty of Peace and Alliance between England and the Netherlands is signed at Whitehall, London
Bishop Gore School, one of the oldest schools in Wales, founded
French officer Louis-Armand de Lom d’Arce, baron Lahontan arrives at Fort Saint-Joseph (modern Detroit) as its new commander (a year later abandons and razes to the ground)
1st lighthouse in American colonies lit at Boston Harbor
Austrian troops occupy Dresden
Peace of Adrianopel: ends Russian-Turkish war
US Marines under General Scott enter Mexico City (halls of Montezuma)
Alexander Stewart opens the 1st US department store, “The Marble Palace” in downtown New York City
Samuel D Hubbard of Conn takes office as 18th US Postmaster General
British and French forces land at Calamita Bay on the Crimean Peninsula, during the Crimean War
Battle of San Jacinto at Hacienda San Jacinto, Nicaraguan forces defeat American filibusters
Battle at Crampton's Gap: Union troops win a tactical victory over Confederate forces
Battle at South Mountain: Union troops defeat an outnumbered Confederate force
Battle of Munfordville, Kentucky, begins
Federal troops escape from beleaguered Harpers Ferry, West Virginia
Skirmish at Fox's Gap, American Civil War
Britain pays US$15½m for damages during Civil War
British General Wolseley reaches Cairo
George K Anderson of Memphis, Tennessee, patents typewriter ribbon
"Empire State Express" train goes from NYC to East Buffalo, a distance of 436 miles, in a record 7H6M
Hottentotten uprising in Southwest Africa fails
Henry Bliss becomes 1st recorded US death from an auto accident when he is hit by a taxicab in New York City
There are now 62,000 foreign troops in Peking and nearby cities, still defeating Boxer Rebels.
Albert Cuypstrat street market in Amsterdam inaugurated
Dutch AR-politician AWF Idenburg named governor of Suriname
RAC Tourist Trophy first run on Isle of Man
Russian Prime Minister Peter Stolypin is assassinated in Kiev; his regime had been characterized by harsh measures to control dissidents
Cubs Larry Cheney hurls record 14-hit shutout against Giants (7-0)
German staff-of-chief Helmut von Moltke replaced by Erich von Falkenhayn
German troops withdraw from Aisne
WWI: Austria-Hungary sends a note to the Allies requesting peace discussions, but the Allies reject the offer
British regime forbids Sinn Féin Dáil
The Trial of Sacco & Vanzetti: Sacco and Vanzetti indicted for murder
Burning of Smyrna, the Asia Minor Catastrophe to Greeks: Ottoman army expels Greeks and other non-Turks from Asia Minor, destroying much of the port city of Smyrna (August 13 OS)
Red Sox 1st baseman George Burns pulls off an unassisted triple play
A's clinch AL pennant with a 5-0 win over White Sox
Detroit Lions (as Portsmouth Spartans) play 1st NFL game, win 13-6
Nazis gain 107 seats in German election
Military coup in Chile led by Arturo Alessandri
2 billion board feet of lumber destroyed in Tillamook Oregon fire
First prefrontal lobotomy in America performed by Walter Freeman and James W. Watts at George Washington University Hospital in Washington, D.C.
British fleet attacks German U-39 boat
German bomb hits shelter in Chelsea; hundreds die
US Congress passes 1st peace-time conscription bill (draft law)
Battle of Edson's Ridge (Japanese assault) at Guadalcanal
German troops occupy train station Stalingrad-1
Yanks clinch pennant #13
Yanks clinch pennant #14
6,500 Dutch/Indonesian captives sent to Junyo Maru
Great Atlantic hurricane hits New England, 300-400 die along the US East Coast
Gulpen, Meerssen & Maastricht freed
US 28th Infantry division occupies 1.5 km of Roscheid
US 4th Ivy League Inf division pushes through Westwall
Groundbreaking ceremony for initial buildings of United Nations Headquarters is held in New York City
Groundbreaking ceremony for UN world headquarters in New York
India's Constituent Assembly adopts Hindi as an official language, celebrated today as Hindi Day
Western allies rearm West Germany
Giant's Bob Niemans homers on his 1st 2 at bats
Yanks clinch 5th straight pennant with 8-5 win over Indians
Hurricane Edna (2nd of 1954) hits NYC, $50 million damage
USSR performs nuclear test
MLB Cleveland Indians pitcher Herb Score sets rookie record of 235 strikeouts; finishes season with 245
Great Britain performs nuclear test at Maralinga, Australia
UN resolution deplores and condemns the Soviet invasion of Hungary
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Two rockets designed by the German engineer Ernst Mohr, the first German post-war rockets, reach the upper atmosphere
WTAE TV channel 4 in Pittsburgh, PA (ABC) begins broadcasting
Soviet Union's Luna 2 is the first spacecraft to impact the Moon
WQEX TV channel 16 in Pittsburgh, PA (PBS) begins broadcasting
Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela form the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries (OPEC)
KERA TV channel 13 in Dallas, TX (PBS) begins broadcasting
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Mary Ann Fischer of Aberdeen, South Dakota, gives birth to America's 1st surviving quintuplets, 4 girls & a boy
WCVE TV channel 23 in Richmond, VA (PBS) begins broadcasting
4th meeting of 2nd Vatican council opened
Western sitcom "F-Troop" premieres in the US on ABC
Melville Abrams Ball Field in Bronx named
Thomas Pell Wildlife Refuge & Sanctuary opens in The Bronx, NYC
Dmitri Shostakovich' 12th string quartet, premieres in Moscow
MLB Detroit Tigers'Denny McLain's 30th victory of season
USSR's Zond 5 is launched on 1st circumlunar flight
Male voters of Swiss Canton Schaffhausen reject female suffrage
Economic Council for Northern Ireland holds its first meeting
Cleveland Indians & Washington Senators, play 20 innings
Two British soldiers are killed in separate shooting incidents in County Tyrone, Northern Ireland
"The Waltons" TV program premieres on CBS starring Richard Thomas, Ralph Waite, Michael Learned, and Will Geer
2 people are killed and 1 mortally wounded in a Ulster Volunteer Force bomb attack on the Imperial Hotel, Belfast
Jason Miller's "That Championship Season," premieres in NYC
West Germany & Poland establish diplomatic relations
Indianapolis is awarded a WHA franchise
Israel shoots down 13 Syrian MIG-21s
Charles Kowal discovers Leda, 13th satellite of Jupiter
Milwaulkee Brewer Robin Yount breaks Mel Ott's record, playing in 242 MLB games as a teen
Rembrandt's oil painting "The Night Watch" slashed & damaged by an unemployed school teacher, in the Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam
Christmas Tinto sentenced to 7 years in Robben Island, South Africa
Portugal government of Da Costa falls
The Provisional Irish Republican Army explode over 50 bombs in towns across Northern Ireland over the next 5 days, injuring 37 people
The film "Quadrophenia", loosely based on The Who's 1973 rock opera of the same name, is released
Theodore Coombs completes 5,193 mile roller skate from LA to NYC & back to Yates Center, Ks
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Dwight Clarkbegins NFL streak of 105 consecutive game receptions
Entertainment Tonight premieres on TV
Judge Wapner & People's Court premiere on TV
36" snow (Red Lodge, MT)
Bomb at Lebanese Phalange party HQ kills President-elect Bachir Gemayel and 26 others
Cindy Nicholas of Canada makes her 19th swim of English Channel
Trevor Baxter sets skateboard high jump record of 5' 5.7"
Texan San Jacinto county sheriff James "Humpy" Parker convicted of violating inmates civil rights and of torture
US House of Representatives votes, 416 to 0, in favor of a resolution condemning Russia for shooting down a Korean jetliner
Bomb attack in Paris, 2 killed
Bomb explosion on airport Kimpo at Seoul, 5 killed
Saskatchewan & Hamilton play 1st CFL regular-season overtime game
Toronto Blue Jays hit a record 10 HRs vs Baltimore Orioles
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Calgary Flames become 1st NHL team to play in USSR, win 4-2
Jeff Reardon is 1st to record 30 saves in 5 consecutive seasons
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Ken Griffey, Sr & Jr, hit back-to-back HRs in 1st inning
San Diego State freshman running back Marshall Faulk rushes for NCAA record 386 yards and scores 7 touchdowns as the Aztecs beat Pacific, 55-34
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
First subway car is completed for export from the US to Taiwan
Body Worlds opens in Tokyo, Japan
As'Mark McGwireis 13th player to hit 50-HRs in a season
England Test cricket fast bowler Dean Headley equals WR by taking his 3rd hat-trick of the English County season for Kent v Hampshire at Canterbury
NY Met Todd Huntley sets record of 41 HRs by a catcher
49th Emmy Awards: Law & Order, Frasier, Dennis Franz & Gillian Anderson win
Chicago Whites Sox retire Carlton Fisk's #72
British TV show "The Royle Family" written and starring Caroline Adherne and Craig Cash, also starring Ricky Tomlinson and Sue Johnston premieres on BBC Two
MCI Communications and WorldCom complete their $37 billion merger to form the telecommunications giant MCI WorldCom
Kiribati, Nauru and Tonga join the United Nations
Baltimore running back Jamal Lewis sets the NFL single-game rushing record when he runs for 295 yards and 2 touchdowns on 30 carries in the Ravens 33-13 victory over Cleveland
Estonia votes to join the European Union in a referendum
Sweden rejects adopting the Euro in a referendum
Ferry sinks amid 10 foot waves off Indonesia's Sumatra Island, killing 21 people
The United States defeats Serbia 129-92 to win the 2014 FIBA Basketball World Cup
19 people die when a boat capsizes on Yamun River in Uttar Pradesh, India
Bodleian Library reveals earliest evidence of the zero symbol in 3rd or 4th Century Bakhshali (Pakistan) manuscript, through carbon dating
Fire at a religious school in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, kills 23
Two Islamic State terrorist attacks in Dhiqar province, Southern Iraq, kill at least 60
Former Trump campaign chair Paul Manafort pleads guilty to conspiracy charges and agrees to co-operative with Government investigations
Hurricane Florence makes landfall near Wrightsville Beach, North Carolina, as a category 1 hurricane
Drone attack on Abqaiq oil plant in Saudi Arabia takes out half of country's oil production and 5% of the world's. Yemen Houthi rebels claim responsibility.
Former Welsh rugby union and league player Gareth Thomas reveals he is HIV positive
Astronomers report possible sign of life on Venus after detecting phosphine in the planet's atmosphere by telescope
Israel becomes the 1st country to announce a second national lockdown due to COVID-19, for 3 weeks
Jakarta, Indonesia's biggest city of 10 million, imposes widespread restrictions again, amid warnings the healthcare system is on the verge of collapse
WHO reports largest-ever one-day COVID-19 case rise of 307,930, daily death toll of 5,500, overall death total is 917,417
1 in 500 Americans has died of COVID-19 as the nation's known death toll reaches 663,913 (Johns Hopkins)
US records lowest level of people living in poverty since records began in 1967 (9.1% vs 11.8% in 2019), due to increase in government aid
Huge loss of life in Libyan floods could have been avoided with functioning weather service, according to World Meteorological Organization, amid reports death toll could reach 20,000
Hunter Biden, son of US President Joe Biden, indicted on charges tied to the possession of a gun while using narcotics
NASA probe into UFO sightings inconclusive, largely due to lack of good data - found no evidence of aliens but doesn't rule out the possibility
US Navy commissions its first mixed-gender submarine the USS New Jersey at Naval Weapons Station Earle, Middletown, New Jersey
Domitianbecomes Emperor of the Roman Empire upon the death of his brotherTitus
Siege of 's-Hertogenbosch: Spanish garrison surrenders toFrederick Henry, Prince of Orange
George Frideric Handelfinishes his "Messiah" oratorio after working on it nonstop for 24 days
Aaron Burracquitted of a misdemeanor charge
Karl Marxpublishes "Das Kapital" Volume 1, his theory of the Capitalist system and how it is doomed to destroy itself
Belgian KingLeopold IIcloses the Brussels Geographic Conference, establishes the International African Association, forerunner of Leopold's privately controlled Congo Free State
Henry Morton Stanley's expedition leaves Rwanda
Theodore Rooseveltis sworn in as the youngest man to serve as US President, after William McKinley finally dies after an anarchist shoots him in Buffalo
Lord Kitchener: "Your country needs you" appears as front cover design for the London Opinion magazine
Christy Mathewsonpitches & wins his final game
In his 4th heavyweight boxing title defenceJack Dempseyrecovers after being sent through the ropes to KO Argentine challenger Luis Firpo in the 2nd round at the Polo Grounds, NYC
Walter Johnsonselected as the American League MVP
Pittsburgh Pirates' future Baseball Hall of Fame outfielder Paul Waner tiesRogers Hornsby's NL record of 200 MLB hits for 7th time
PresidentFranklin D. Rooseveltbans US ships from trading arms with China or Japan
First Lord of the AdmiraltyWinston Churchillvisits British naval base Scapa Flow in the Orkney Islands
World's first practical helicopter, the VS-300, designed byIgor Sikorsky, takes a short tethered flight in Stratford, Connecticut
Gerald Fordupsets Representative Bartel J Jonkman in Michigan 5th Dist Republican primary
Nikita Khrushchevappointed First Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union, succeedingMalenkov
Benjamin Britten's opera "Turn of the Screw" premieres in Venice, Italy
New York GiantsWillie Maysgets 82nd extra-base hit, breaksMel Ott's record
Little Richardrecords "Tutti Frutti" at J & M Studio in New Orleans, Louisiana, kicking off the tune with the immortal phrase "A-wop-bop-a-loo-mop-a-lop-bam-boom"
NY Yankees win 24th pennant and 9th underCasey Stengel
Dmitri Shostakovichbecomes member of Communist Party of USSR
Joseph-Desiré Mobutu launches a bloodless coup d'état in the Congo, declaring PresidentJoseph Kasa-VubuandPatrice Lumumbato be "neutralised" and establishing a new government of university graduates
PresidentLyndon Johnsonpresents journalistWalter Lippmannwith the Presidential Medal of Freedom
Walt Disneyawarded Presidential Medal of Freedom at White House by PresidentLyndon Johnson
Jimmy Ellis beatsFloyd Pattersonin 15 for heavyweight boxing title
While on tour in England, a fire at singerRoy Orbison's Henderson, Tennessee home kills the two eldest of his three sons
US PresidentRichard Nixonsigns into law a measure lifting pro football's blackout
MotherElizabeth Ann Bayley Setoncanonized as 1st US-born saint by PopePaul VI
MLB Atlanta Braves'Jim Bouton(38) beats San Francisco Giants, his 1st win since 1970
"The Golden Girls", starringBea Arthur,Betty White, Rue McClanahan, andEstelle Getty, debuts on NBC
Bo Jackson's 1st HR-a 475-foot blast (longest at Royal Stadium)
CBS premiere of true life "The Last Days ofPatton"

NFL Bears running backWalter Paytonscores his 100th career rushing touchdown, and gains 177 yards, surpassing 15,000-yard career plateau in 13-10 overtime win over visiting Philadelphia Eagles, at Soldier Field, Chicago, Illinois
Cal Ripken Jr.'s record streak of 8,243 consecutive innings (908 games) is finally broken

MCA and Virgin Records release album "Bat Out of Hell II: Back into Hell" by singerMeat Loaf, written and produced byJim Steinman; it arrives 16 years after the first installment of eventual trilogy, and features the hit "I'd Do Anything for Love (But I Won't Do That)"
Restrictions on the traditional Latin Mass are officially removed in the Roman Catholic Church as PopeBenedict XVI's motu proprio Summorum Pontificum takes effect
David AttenboroughandPrince Williamofficially open the Darwin Centre at the National History Museum London, in what is the Museum's most significant expansion since 1881
Elizabeth Warrenannounces she intends to run for the Democratic nomination for the 2012 Massachusetts Senatorial election
14-year-old Texan Ahmed Mohamed is arrested at school when a homemade clock is assumed to be a bomb;Mark Zuckerbergand US PresidentBarack Obamasend supportive tweets
Malcolm Turnbullousts Tony Abbott as Australian Prime Minister and leader of the ruling Liberal Party
Selena Gomezreveals she had a kidney transplant, because of lupus, donated by her friend Francia Rais
California GovernorGavin Newsomdefeats a state vote to recall him from office
Florida GovernorRon DeSantissend two planes of Venezuelan immigrants to Martha's Vineyard, Massachusetts, as a political ploy
MusicianR. Kellyfound convicted of further sex crimes in Chicago, including producing child sexual abuse imagery
Procession ofQueen Elizabeth II's coffin from Buckingham Palace to the Palace of Westminster to lie in state, with queues of people paying their respects forming 2.4 miles (3.8km)
Britain and the British Empire, including the American colonies, adopt the Gregorian Calendar after skipping 11 days between September 3 and September 13
Great Fire of Moscow begins asNapoleonapproaches the city and retreating Russians burn it - fire continues to burn for five days
Francis Scott Key pens the poem "Defence of Fort M'Henry," later known as "The Star-Spangled Banner," while witnessing the bombardment of Fort McHenry from a ship in Baltimore Harbor
Graf Zeppelin II, world's largest airship, makes its maiden flight

US Navy AdmiralChester Nimitzpresents the 1st Medal of Honor of WWII, for courage and valor beyond the call of duty during the attack on Pearl Harbor, to sailor John William Finn; ceremony took place in Pearl Harbor aboard USS Enterprise
IBM introduces the RAMAC 305, the first commercial computer with a hard drive with magnetic disk storage, weighs over a ton

Historic National Prayer Service held at Washington National Cathedral for victims of the September 11 attacks. A similar service is held in Canada on Parliament Hill, the largest vigil ever held in the nation's capital.
Al-Ma'mun, 7th Caliph of the Abbasid Caliphate (813-833), born in Baghdad (d. 833)
Claudius Clavus, Danish cartographer (probably 1st to draw Greenland on a map), born in Salling, Island of Fünen (date of death unknown)

Eric Bentley, British-American critic and writer (In Search of Theater), born in Bolton, Lancashire, England (d. 2020)
